","type":"text/x-markdown"},"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/llm-help/README.md":{"title":"/home/runner/work/tw-plugin-library/tw-plugin-library/wiki/plugins/rimir/llm-help/README.md","text":"# LLM Help A self-discoverable, hierarchical help system that lets LLMs browse context-aware documentation to solve wiki-specific tasks autonomously. ## How it works The plugin provides a single MCP tool `llm_help` that LLMs call to navigate a tree of help pages: - `llm_help()` — returns top-level help categories - `llm_help("data-model")` — navigates deeper into a category - `llm_help("data-model/person/create")` — reaches a specific help page Each level returns child topics with descriptions, so the LLM decides where to go based on its current task. Help pages can contain dynamic content — rendered at call time from the wiki's live state. ## Key features - **Hierarchical navigation** — help pages organized in a tree of any depth - **Dynamic content** — pages rendered as wikitext at call time, pulling live data from the wiki - **Context-aware** — pages can be scoped to specific contexts (e.g., an appify app) - **Multi-path mounting** — same page reachable from multiple paths in the tree - **Plugin-extensible** — any plugin can contribute help pages via tagged tiddlers - **User shortcuts** — users can add quick-reference help pages via the settings UI - **Context selector** — settings popup to toggle active context keys with page counts - **Help browser** — settings tab lets users see and navigate what the LLM sees ## For plugin authors To add help pages from your plugin, create shadow tiddlers with: - Tag: `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` - Field `llm-help-path`: navigation path (space-separated for multiple paths) - Field `llm-help-description`: one-line description shown in parent listings - Optional `llm-help-sort`: numeric sort order within siblings - Optional `llm-help-context`: space-separated context keys ## Requirements - `$:/plugins/rimir/llm-connect` — provides the MCP tool infrastructure - `$:/plugins/rimir/theme` — provides UI styling classes ## License MIT ","type":"text/x-markdown"},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/changelog":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/changelog","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"|!Version |!Date |!Changes | |0.1.30 |2026-04-27 |Declare `doc-template` as soft-dependent in plugin.info; <%else%> (no help context active) <%endif%> "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/architecture":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/architecture","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","rdt.order":"2","rdt.plugin-key":"llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions","rdt.section":"Architecture","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page"> <$fill $name="body"> !! Components * ''`functions.tid`'' -- shared filter functions for path resolution, child discovery, context filtering. Used by both the tool and the browser UI. * ''`tool.tid`'' -- the `llm_help` MCP tool, tagged `$:/tags/rimir/llm-connect/tool`. Renders wikitext pages and lists children. * ''`settings.tid`'' -- help browser UI with breadcrumb navigation, content area, child cards, and user shortcut management. * ''`styles.tid`'' -- CSS for the browser UI components. !! Help page tiddler convention Every help page is a tiddler tagged `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` with fields: |!Field |!Required |!Description | |`llm-help-path` |yes |Space-separated paths (e.g., `data-model/person/create actions/create/person`) | |`llm-help-description` |yes |One-line description shown in parent listings | |`llm-help-sort` |no |Numeric sort within siblings (default: alphabetical) | |`llm-help-context` |no |Space-separated context keys for visibility filtering | !! Path resolution Paths are stored in the `llm-help-path` field, not derived from tiddler titles. This allows any plugin to mount content at any path while keeping tiddlers under its own namespace. Multi-path: A page with `llm-help-path: data-model/person/create actions/create/person` appears at both paths. !! Child discovery For a given path, the system: # Collects all visible pages' paths via `_llm-help-all-paths` # Filters to paths starting with `/` # Extracts the next path segment after the prefix # Deduplicates and sorts Virtual parent nodes are synthesized automatically when children exist but no explicit page does. !! Context filtering Context uses hierarchical prefix matching: a page is visible if it has no `llm-help-context` (always visible) or if any of its context keys are a prefix of any active context key. For example, active context `rimir/appify` makes pages with context `rimir/appify`, `rimir/appify/channels`, or `rimir/appify/views` visible. For tool calls, active context comes from `$:/temp/rimir/llm-help/active-context` (synced from the per-chat `llm-help-context` field before each tool execution) or the tool's optional `context` parameter. The settings browser uses its own `$:/state/rimir/llm-help/browser/context` tiddler (defaults to all keys, independent from chats). !! Dynamic content All pages are wikitext, rendered at tool-call time via the llm-connect tool executor. Pages can use `$importvariables`, `$list`, `$let`, filters, etc. to read schemas, enumerate instances, or compute anything from the wiki store. Example: The orga plugin's type pages import `typed/functions.tid` and render the live schema for each type, including field definitions, constraints, and existing instances. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/dev-workflow":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/dev-workflow","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","rdt.order":"5","rdt.plugin-key":"llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions","rdt.section":"Dev-Workflow","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page"> <$fill $name="body"> !! Adding help pages from your plugin # Create `.tid` files under `your-plugin/llm-help/` # Tag each with `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` # Set `llm-help-path` and `llm-help-description` fields # The pages are discovered automatically when the plugin is installed !! Restart rules * Help page tiddlers (`.tid` files): ''no restart needed'' — they are shadow tiddlers read from the wiki store * The `functions.tid` and `tool.tid` are also shadow tiddlers — ''no restart needed'' for content changes * Changes to `settings.tid` (UI): ''no restart needed'' !! Testing help pages # Start the wiki server # Open Settings > LLM Help to browse the help tree # Verify your pages appear at the expected paths # Check that dynamic content renders correctly # Test the `llm_help` tool in a chat session !! Conventions * Keep help content concise and LLM-readable — avoid complex wikitext formatting * Use `$text` and `$list` widgets for dynamic content (they work in the tool executor's fakeDocument) * Don't use `$reveal` or DOM-dependent widgets in help page content * Use `$importvariables` to pull in functions from other plugins when needed * Put shared procedures in a separate macro tiddler (like `type-info-macro.tid`) "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/examples":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/examples","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","rdt.order":"4","rdt.plugin-key":"llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions","rdt.section":"Examples","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page"> <$fill $name="body"> !! Creating a help page (static) ``` title: $:/plugins/rimir/my-plugin/llm-help/my-topic tags: $:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page llm-help-path: my-plugin/my-topic llm-help-description: Description shown in parent listings type: text/vnd.tiddlywiki Content the LLM will read when it navigates to this path. ``` !! Multi-path page Mount the same content at two paths: ``` llm-help-path: data-model/person/create actions/create/person ``` The LLM reaches this page whether it navigates via `data-model` or `actions/create`. !! Dynamic page (reading domain schema) ``` title: $:/plugins/your-plugin/llm-help/type-person tags: $:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page llm-help-path: data-model/person llm-help-description: Person type — fields, constraints, creation, search type: text/vnd.tiddlywiki <$importvariables filter="[[$:/plugins/your-plugin/llm-help/type-info-macro]]"> <> ``` The macro can read your plugin's schema definitions and render field info dynamically. !! Context-restricted page Only visible when the active context includes `rimir/appify`: ``` llm-help-context: rimir/appify ``` Only visible in a specific app: ``` llm-help-context: orga-view ``` !! User shortcut Created via the settings UI or manually: ``` title: $:/llm-help/user/create-person-shortcut tags: $:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page llm-help-path: shortcuts/create-person llm-help-description: Quick guide to creating a person type: text/vnd.tiddlywiki 1. Find the parent org: search_tiddlers with rrt.type=orga 2. Create person: create_tiddler with basetitle=/pn: 3. Set fields: caption, abbrev, parent, status ``` "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/faq":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/faq","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","rdt.order":"6","rdt.plugin-key":"llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions","rdt.section":"FAQ","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page"> <$fill $name="body"> !! Can two plugins register pages at the same path? Yes. The system uses `first[]` — the first tiddler found wins (shadow tiddler ordering is by title). Users can override by creating a non-shadow tiddler with the same tag and path. !! How does multi-path work? The `llm-help-path` field accepts space-separated paths. A page with `llm-help-path: a/b c/d` appears as a child of both `a` and `c`. The LLM navigates to either path and sees the same content. !! What happens when a page has children but no content? The tool lists the children with their descriptions. A "virtual parent" node is synthesized — no explicit tiddler needed at intermediate levels. !! How does context filtering work? Pages with no `llm-help-context` are always visible. Pages with `llm-help-context: rimir/appify/channels` are visible when the active context includes `rimir/appify` (or `rimir/appify/channels` exactly). Matching is hierarchical: an active context key matches any page context key that starts with it. The active context is a space-separated list of keys. !! Can users edit or override plugin-provided help pages? Yes. Create a regular tiddler (not shadow) with the same tag and a matching `llm-help-path`. Non-shadow tiddlers take precedence over shadow tiddlers in TiddlyWiki. !! Why doesn't the root page use `_llm-help-at`? The root page has an empty `llm-help-path` value. Since `enlist-input[]` on an empty string returns nothing, `_llm-help-at` can't match it. The root is found via `_llm-help-root-page` which checks for blank path directly. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/overview":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/overview","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","rdt.hub-category":"integration","rdt.hub-category-name":"Integration","rdt.hub-order":"2","rdt.order":"1","rdt.plugin-key":"llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions","rdt.section":"Overview","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page"> <$fill $name="body"> !! What it does llm help provides a self-discoverable, hierarchical documentation system that LLMs navigate via a single MCP tool `llm_help`. Instead of loading all documentation upfront, the LLM browses a tree of help pages level by level, reading only what it needs for the current task. !! Features * ''Hierarchical navigation'' -- help pages organized in a tree of any depth, navigated level by level * ''Dynamic content'' -- pages are wikitext rendered at call time, pulling live data from the wiki (e.g., typed schemas, existing instances) * ''Context-aware'' -- pages can be scoped to specific contexts (e.g., an appify app) via hierarchical prefix matching * ''Multi-path mounting'' -- same page reachable from multiple paths in the tree via space-separated `llm-help-path` values * ''Plugin-extensible'' -- any plugin contributes help pages by tagging tiddlers with `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` * ''User shortcuts'' -- users add quick-reference help pages via the settings UI for common tasks or failing use cases * ''Help browser'' -- settings tab lets users see and navigate the exact same tree the LLM sees !! Prerequisites * [[$:/plugins/rimir/llm-connect]] -- provides the MCP tool infrastructure * [[$:/plugins/rimir/theme]] -- provides UI styling classes !! Quick start # Install the plugin (requires llm-connect and theme) # The `llm_help` tool automatically appears in the llm-connect tool list # Open Settings > LLM Help to browse the help tree # Other plugins (e.g., orga) contribute domain-specific help pages automatically !! How the LLM uses it ``` llm_help() → see top-level topics llm_help("data-model") → navigate into data model docs llm_help("data-model/person") → read person schema (dynamic) llm_help("data-model/person/create") → step-by-step creation guide ``` Each response includes subtopic listings with descriptions, so the LLM autonomously decides where to navigate based on the task at hand. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/reference":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/reference","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","rdt.order":"3","rdt.plugin-key":"llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions","rdt.section":"Reference","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page"> <$fill $name="body"> !! Help page fields |!Field |!Required |!Type |!Description | |`llm-help-path` |yes |string |Space-separated list of paths. Each path is `/`-delimited (e.g., `data-model/person/create`). | |`llm-help-description` |yes |string |One-line summary shown when this page appears in a parent's subtopic listing. | |`llm-help-sort` |no |string |Numeric sort key for ordering among siblings. Pages without sort are ordered alphabetically by path segment. | |`llm-help-context` |no |string |Space-separated context keys. If set, page is only visible when active context intersects. If absent, page is always visible. | !! Tag All help pages must be tagged: `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` !! Tool schema ```json { "type": "object", "properties": { "path": { "type": "string", "description": "Help path. Empty for root." }, "context": { "type": "string", "description": "Optional context override (space-separated keys)." } } } ``` !! Filter functions (`$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/functions`) |!Function |!Parameters |!Returns | |`_llm-help-all-pages` |none |All help page tiddler titles | |`_llm-help-ctx-match` |`page-ctx, ctx` |First page context key that prefix-matches any active context key (helper) | |`_llm-help-ctx-pages` |`ctx` |Pages with `llm-help-context` matching ctx (hierarchical prefix match) | |`_llm-help-visible-pages` |`ctx` |Pages visible in given context (context-free + context-matched) | |`_llm-help-resolve-ctx` |`ctx` |Effective context (param or state tiddler) | |`_llm-help-root-page` |`ctx` |Root page tiddler (blank path) | |`_llm-help-at` |`path, ctx` |Page tiddler at exact path | |`_llm-help-all-paths` |`ctx` |All individual paths from visible pages | |`_llm-help-child-segments` |`prefix, ctx` |Unique next-level path segments | !! State tiddlers |!Tiddler |!Purpose | |`$:/temp/rimir/llm-help/active-context` |Active context keys for tool calls (space-separated). Synced from per-chat `llm-help-context` field before tool execution. Transient — resets on reload. | |`$:/state/rimir/llm-help/browser/context` |Settings browser context (space-separated). Independent from chats. Defaults to all keys on first use. | |`$:/state/rimir/llm-help/browser/path` |Current path in the settings help browser. | !! User shortcut tiddlers User-created shortcuts live at `$:/llm-help/user/` and are tagged `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` with `llm-help-path: shortcuts/`. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/tw-internals":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/doc/tw-internals","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","rdt.order":"7","rdt.plugin-key":"llm-help","rdt.plugin-name":"llm help","rdt.plugin-subtitle":"Self-discoverable hierarchical help system for LLM interactions","rdt.section":"Tw-Internals","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"<$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/rimir/doc-template/templates/page"> <$fill $name="body"> !! Shadow tiddler discovery Help pages from plugins are shadow tiddlers. The discovery filter `[all[shadows+tiddlers]tag[...]]` finds both shadow and regular tiddlers. Regular tiddlers (user overrides) take precedence over shadows with the same title. !! Filter function mechanics Functions defined with `\function` are stored as tiddler text and evaluated via `[function[name],,]`. The llm-help functions chain multiple filter runs: * `:filter[subfilter]` — keeps items where the subfilter returns non-empty output. Inside the subfilter, `` refers to each item being tested. * Multiple `[runs]` inside `:filter[...]` are unioned — used for OR logic (e.g., blank context OR matching context). * `+[unique[sort[]]]` — deduplicates and sorts the accumulated output. !! `enlist-input[]` for multi-value fields The `llm-help-path` field contains space-separated paths. The operator chain `get[llm-help-path]enlist-input[]` first reads the raw field value, then splits it into individual items. This is how multi-path mounting works — each path becomes a separate item in the filter pipeline. !! Tool execution context The `llm_help` tool runs inside llm-connect's tool executor (`tool-executor.js`), which: * Renders the tool's wikitext body via `$tw.wiki.makeWidget()` with `$tw.fakeDocument` * Injects tool input parameters as widget variables (e.g., `path`, `context`) * Collects the rendered text output (strips HTML tags) * Truncates at `max-tool-result-length` (default 4000 chars) This means help page content must use server-compatible widgets (`$list`, `$let`, `$text`, `$importvariables`, `<%if%>`). DOM-dependent widgets (`$reveal` with animations, `$scrollable`) won't work. !! `$importvariables` for cross-plugin functions Dynamic help pages can use `<$importvariables filter="[[$:/plugins/your-plugin/functions]]">` to pull in filter functions from another plugin. This is necessary because `\import` only accepts literal tiddler references, not computed filters. Despite being deprecated, `$importvariables` remains the only way to dynamically scope functions at render time. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-cascades":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-cascades","created":"20260407120000000","modified":"20260407120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/cascades","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/cascades","llm-help-description":"ViewTemplateBodyFilter :cascade ordering — buggy third-party filters break subsequent entries","llm-help-sort":"06","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"ViewTemplateBodyFilter Cascade Ordering The `:cascade` filter run prefix evaluates step filters sequentially. If a third-party plugin's cascade filter has a bug (error, side effect), it can silently prevent ALL subsequent cascade filters from matching. Symptom: your cascade filter works when tested alone but returns empty when run as part of the full chain: ``` {{{ [] :cascade[[$:/my/cascade/filter]get[text]] }}} {{{ [] :cascade[all[shadows+tiddlers]tag[$:/tags/ViewTemplateBodyFilter]!is[draft]get[text]] }}} ``` Known offenders: `flibbles/graph`, `wikilabs`, `EvidentlyCube/AutoComplete` cascade filters have been observed to break the chain in TW 5.3.8. Fix: position your cascade filter FIRST, not last: ``` list-before: $:/config/ViewTemplateBodyFilters/default list-before: $:/config/ViewTemplateBodyFilters/testcase ``` Also: `all[current]` is redundant in `:cascade` step filters. The cascade prefix already passes the tiddler title as input to each step filter: ``` [all[current]has[_thumbnail_uri]then[...]] [has[_thumbnail_uri]then[...]] ``` "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-filter-runs":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-filter-runs","created":"20260329120000000","modified":"20260329120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/filter-runs","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/filter-runs","llm-help-description":"Filter run composition — ~, +, :filter scope, :map single-result, interleaving, conditional operators","llm-help-sort":"06","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Filter Run Composition Gotchas These gotchas are about how multiple filter runs interact. Understanding `~`, `+`, `:filter` scope, and space-separated runs is critical for building dynamic lists. --- 1. `~` (else) only fires on truly empty output — not on "0" or falsy values The `~` prefix means "use this run only if ALL previous runs produced no output at all". It does NOT check for "0", empty strings, or falsy values — only for zero items in the result set. `+[count[]]` ALWAYS produces output (the string `"0"` or `"5"` etc.), so `~` after it NEVER fires. ``` [rrt.type[project]status[active]] +[count[]] ~[[0]] [rrt.type[project]status[planned]] +[count[]] ~[] <$let total={{{[rrt.type[project]status[active]count[]]}}}> <%if [match[0]]%>No projects found.<%endif%> <$list filter="[rrt.type[project]status[active]]" emptyMessage="No projects found."> ... ``` Rule: Never use `~` after `+[count[]]`. Use `$list emptyMessage` or `$let` + `<%if%>` for empty-state handling. --- 2. `:filter` applies to ALL accumulated items, not just the last run When you have multiple space-separated runs followed by `:filter`, the filter applies to every item accumulated so far — including items from earlier runs. ``` [[sec:osaca]] [rrt.type[project]field:parent[work/mgm/osaca]] :filter[get[status]match[active]] <$let filtered-projects={{{[rrt.type[project]field:parent[work/mgm/osaca]status[active]join[ ]]}}}> [[sec:osaca]] [enlist] [rrt.type[project]field:parent[work/mgm/osaca]status[active]] ``` Rule: `:filter`, `:map`, `:sort`, `:reduce` are run-level operations that act on the ENTIRE accumulated result set. If you need to filter only some items, do it within a single run's operators or pre-compute with `$let`. --- 3. `+` replaces input — it does NOT append The `+` prefix takes the accumulated results as input to the new run and replaces the output. It is a pipe, not a union. ``` [[sec:osaca]] +[rrt.type[project]field:parent[work/mgm/osaca]] [[sec:osaca]] [rrt.type[project]field:parent[work/mgm/osaca]] ``` Run prefix cheat sheet: - `[run]` or `[run]` (space-separated) — UNION: adds results to the output - `+[run]` — PIPE: takes accumulated output as input, replaces output with result - `-[run]` — SUBTRACT: removes results of this run from accumulated output - `~[run]` — ELSE: only runs if all previous output is empty - `:filter[run]` — FILTER: keeps only items where the sub-filter produces output - `:map[run]` — MAP: transforms each item, replacing it with the sub-filter result --- 4. Interleaving items from different sources requires wikitext, not filters Building a flat list like `section1 item1 item2 section2 item3` where sections and items come from different queries is extremely difficult in a single filter expression. Filters are good at flat lists from one source — not at interleaving. ``` [rrt.type[project]each[parent]get[parent]] :map[ addprefix[sec:] ...somehow append children... ] <$list filter="[rrt.type[project]each[parent]get[parent]sort[]]" variable="parent">
<$text text={{{[split[/]last[]]}}}/>
<$list filter="[rrt.type[project]field:parentstatussort[caption]]" variable="proj">
{{!!caption}}
<$vars elements={{{ [[sec:group1]] [source1[group1]] [[sec:group2]] [source2[group2]] +[join[ ]] }}}> <$transclude $tiddler="$:/plugins/your-plugin/tabs" elements=<> .../> ``` Rule: When you need structured/grouped output, use `$list` nesting in wikitext. Reserve single filter expressions for flat, homogeneous lists. --- 5. `:map` returns only the FIRST result per input item — it is NOT a flat-map `:map` replaces each input title with the first output of the subfilter. If the subfilter produces multiple results, all but the first are silently dropped. ``` [get[default-participants]enlist-input[]] :filter[get[rrt.type]match[team]] :map[all[tiddlers]rrt.type[person]contains:team-member-of] [all[tiddlers]rrt.type[person]!status[archived]] :filter[contains:default-participants{!!team-member-of}] ``` Rule: When you need all results for each input (flat-map semantics), reverse the lookup direction. Instead of mapping over containers to find their members, scan all candidates and use `:filter` to check membership against the source list. --- 6. Conditional filter operators don't exist — you can't toggle a filter step on/off A common need: show all items when filter is "all", show only matching items otherwise. You cannot make a single filter operator conditional — there is no `[then[status[active]]]` that injects an operator. ``` [rrt.type[project] <$if condition>status sort[title]] <%if [match[all]]%> <$list filter="[rrt.type[project]field:parentsort[title]]">... <%else%> <$list filter="[rrt.type[project]field:parentstatussort[title]]">... <%endif%> <$let base="[rrt.type[project]field:parent" status-part={{{[!match[all]then[status]]}}} full-filter={{{[addsuffixaddsuffix[sort[title]]]}}}> <$list filter={{{[subfilter]}}} variable="proj">... <$list filter="[rrt.type[project]field:parent] +[match[all]] ~[status]"> ``` Rule: When a filter needs conditional operators, pre-build the filter string with `$let` and `addsuffix`. Don't duplicate `$list` blocks with `<%if%>`/`<%else%>` — it's fragile and verbose. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-filters":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-filters","created":"20260329120000000","modified":"20260329120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/filters","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/filters","llm-help-description":"Filter pitfalls — no short-circuit, :filter negation, get[] emptiness, dot variables, \function syntax","llm-help-sort":"02","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Filter Expression Gotchas 1. Filters do NOT short-circuit In a single run like `[is[blank]then[yes]else{!!field}enlist-input]`, ALL operators execute sequentially. `then[yes]` does NOT stop the chain — `enlist-input` still runs and can wipe out the result. ``` [is[blank]then[yes]else{!!field}enlist-inputmatch] [is[blank]then[yes]] ~[{!!field}enlist-input[]matchthen[yes]] ``` The `~` operator means "use next run only if previous returned empty". --- 2. `:filter` does not support `!` negation Negated operators like `!has[field]` and `!is[blank]` silently fail inside `:filter` — they produce no output, filtering out every item. ``` [tag[X]]:filter[!has[my-field]] [tag[X]!has[my-field]] [tag[X]]:filter[get[my-field]is[blank]then[yes]] ``` --- 3. `get[field]` returns nothing (not "") for missing/empty fields `get[fieldname]` on a missing or empty field returns **no output** — not an empty string. So `is[blank]` after `get` on a missing field has no input and produces nothing. ``` :filter[get[my-field]is[blank]] [!has[my-field]] ``` `has[field]` returns true only when the field exists AND is non-empty. --- 4. Variable names with dots don't work in `<>` syntax `` in a filter does NOT resolve a variable named `f.status`. The dot breaks variable resolution. ``` <$let f.status="active">[match[active]] <$let fStatus="active">[match[active]] ``` --- 5. `\function` body is single-line only Everything after `\function name(params)` on the SAME line is the filter. Continuation lines become visible body text. ``` \function myFunc() [all[tiddlers]tag[X]] \function myFunc() [all[tiddlers]tag[X]] :filter[get[status]match[active]] ``` --- 6. Operator chaining after `function[]` — watch the brackets After `function[name]`, the `]]` looks like a closing run. Chaining operators AFTER `]]` puts them outside the run. ``` [function[myFunc]]count[] [function[myFunc]count[]] [function[myFunc]] +[count[]] ``` --- 7. `` vs `now[]` vs `<>` - `` in a filter — NOT a standard variable, produces wrong results - `<>` — standard TW macro for display - For filter comparison: capture into a variable first with `$let` ``` <> <$let today={{{[]}}}> [compare:date:gt[20260101]] ``` --- 8. `[[` and `]]` cannot be used as literal strings in filters Double square brackets are filter syntax delimiters (title literals). You cannot produce them via `addprefix` or `addsuffix` inside a filter — they get interpreted as filter syntax, not as literal characters. ``` [addprefix[[[]addsuffix[]]]] <!-- CORRECT — store brackets in variables, then reference them --> <$let ob="[[" cb="]]" wrapped={{{ [<title>addprefix<ob>addsuffix<cb>] }}}> <!-- wrapped = "[[My Tiddler]]" as a literal string --> </$let> ``` This commonly comes up when building filter expressions as strings (e.g., for `allowFilter` or dynamic `$list` filters) where tiddler titles need `[[bracket]]` wrapping to handle spaces. --- 9. `subfilter` with literal filter operands — nested brackets break parsing `[subfilter[tag[sensitive]]]` does NOT work. The parser sees `[subfilter[tag[sensitive]` as the operand and `]]` as the closing brackets, producing a malformed filter. ``` <!-- BROKEN — nested brackets confuse the parser --> [subfilter[tag[sensitive]]] <!-- CORRECT — use {tiddler-reference} to read the filter from a tiddler's text field --> [subfilter{$:/config/my-protection-filter}] <!-- CORRECT — use <variable> if filter is in a variable --> <$let myFilter="[tag[sensitive]]"> [subfilter<myFilter>] </$let> ``` This applies to any operator that takes a filter expression as its operand (`subfilter`, `filter`, `reduce`, `map`, etc.). Always use indirection via `{tiddler}` or `<variable>` — never embed a filter literally inside `[]` brackets. --- 10. `jsonget` chain fails on array items — use `jsonextract` first When iterating a JSON array with `:map`, chaining `jsonget<currentTiddler>jsonget[field]` silently returns empty. The second `jsonget` doesn't receive the sub-object. Use `jsonextract` to extract the sub-object, then `jsonget` on the result: ``` <!-- BROKEN — jsonget<idx>jsonget[id] returns empty for every item --> [<cache>get[text]jsonindexes[]] :map[<cache>get[text]jsonget<currentTiddler>jsonget[id]] <!-- CORRECT — jsonextract gets the sub-object, then jsonget reads the field --> [<cache>get[text]jsonindexes[]] :map:flat[<cache>get[text]jsonextract<currentTiddler>jsonget[id]] ``` Note the `:flat` suffix on `:map` — required when the mapped expression returns a single value per item. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-hooks":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-hooks","created":"20260407120000000","modified":"20260407120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/hooks","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/hooks","llm-help-description":"TW hook chain breaks if any hook forgets return tiddler — use change event fallback","llm-help-sort":"07","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Hook Chain Fragility TW's `$tw.hooks.invokeHook` passes each hook's return value as input to the next hook. There is NO try/catch. If any hook returns `undefined` (forgetting `return tiddler`), all subsequent hooks silently receive `undefined` and fail. ```javascript // BROKEN — breaks the chain for all hooks registered after this one $tw.hooks.addHook("th-deleting-tiddler", function(tiddler) { doSomething(tiddler); // Missing: return tiddler; }); // CORRECT $tw.hooks.addHook("th-deleting-tiddler", function(tiddler) { doSomething(tiddler); return tiddler; }); ``` Known offender: `sq/streams` plugin's `th-deleting-tiddler` hook. You cannot control hook registration order reliably (depends on startup module load order). So never rely solely on a hook for critical operations. Robust pattern — two-layer approach: 1. **Hook** (best-effort): show confirm dialogs, record user decisions 2. **Change event** (reliable fallback): `$tw.wiki.addEventListener("change", ...)` fires after every wiki store change, regardless of hooks ```javascript // Snapshot approach: track state BEFORE deletion var snapshot = {}; // title → {data} // Keep snapshot updated via change event $tw.wiki.addEventListener("change", function(changes) { $tw.utils.each(changes, function(change, title) { if(!change.deleted) { // Update snapshot for modified/added tiddlers } else { // Tiddler deleted — use snapshot to act var info = snapshot[title]; delete snapshot[title]; if(info) { /* cleanup */ } } }); }); ``` Draft safety: when a user opens an editor and cancels, the draft tiddler (which inherits fields like `_canonical_uri` from the original) gets deleted. Always check for `draft.of` field: ```javascript // In snapshot building — skip drafts var titles = $tw.wiki.filterTiddlers("[has[_canonical_uri]!has[draft.of]]"); // In change event — skip drafts if(t && t.fields["draft.of"]) return; ``` "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-kin-filter":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-kin-filter","created":"20260408120000000","modified":"20260408120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/kin-filter","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/kin-filter","llm-help-description":"kin filter gotchas — direction semantics, depth off-by-one, global cache staleness, field must be title list","llm-help-sort":"09","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! Kin Filter Gotchas The `[kin[]]` filter (by bimlas) traverses parent-child trees. Syntax: `[kin:<field>:<direction>:<depth>[<operand>]]`. !! 1. Direction semantics are relative to the field, not the tree `from` follows the field value (child→parent), `to` finds things pointing to this title via the field (parent→children), `with` is both. ``` <!-- To find DESCENDANTS of a node (children, grandchildren, etc.) via parent field: --> [kin:parent:to[MyNode]] <!-- NOT: --> [kin:parent:from[MyNode]] <!-- ^^^ this finds ANCESTORS (parent, grandparent, etc.) --> ``` Mnemonic: `to` means "titles pointing TO this one via the field" = descendants. `from` means "where this title's field points FROM" = ancestors. !! 2. The base title is always included in results `[kin:parent:to[MyNode]]` returns MyNode itself plus all descendants. To exclude the base: ``` [kin:parent:to[MyNode]] -[<myNode>] ``` !! 3. Depth parameter is 1-indexed, 0 means unlimited `[kin:parent:to:1[MyNode]]` returns MyNode + its direct children (depth 1). There is no way to say "only depth 1 without the base" in a single operator — subtract the base separately. `[kin:parent:to:0[MyNode]]` and `[kin:parent:to[MyNode]]` are identical — both traverse the full tree. !! 4. Global cache — results can go stale The kin filter caches results globally per (baseTitle, fieldName, direction) tuple. The cache persists for the wiki session. If tiddlers are created/deleted/reparented, cached kin results may be stale until wiki reload. ``` <!-- After creating a new child of MyNode, [kin:parent:to[MyNode]] may NOT include the new child until the page is refreshed --> ``` This primarily affects interactive scenarios (live editing + kin queries in the same session). For static rendering it is not a problem. !! 5. Field must contain a title list The traversed field (default: `tags`) must be a standard TiddlyWiki title list. If a field contains free text or malformed bracket syntax, kin silently skips it. Single-value fields like `parent` work fine since a bare title is a valid title list of one. !! 6. Default field is `tags`, not `parent` If you omit the field suffix, kin uses the `tags` field: ``` <!-- These are DIFFERENT: --> [kin[MyNode]] <!-- traverses tags field --> [kin:parent[MyNode]] <!-- traverses parent field --> ``` Always specify the field explicitly when working with non-tag hierarchies. !! 7. Combines poorly with sort — kin returns discovery order Results come back in traversal order (breadth-first-ish). If you need alphabetical or other sorting, add an explicit sort step: ``` [kin:parent:to[MyNode]] -[<myNode>] +[sort[caption]] ``` "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-shadows":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-shadows","created":"20260329120000000","modified":"20260329120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/shadows","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/shadows","llm-help-description":"Shadow tiddlers from plugins need [all[tiddlers+shadows]] to be found by tag/filter","llm-help-sort":"04","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Shadow Tiddler Discovery Plugin-bundled tiddlers are "shadow" tiddlers. The default `[tag[]]` operator only searches the main wiki store (real tiddlers), NOT the shadow store. ``` <!-- BROKEN — misses shadow tiddlers from plugins --> [tag[$:/tags/MyTag]] <!-- CORRECT — includes both real and shadow tiddlers --> [all[tiddlers+shadows]tag[$:/tags/MyTag]] ``` When to use `[all[tiddlers+shadows]...]`: - Any filter that discovers tiddlers by tag where the tagged tiddlers may come from plugins - Discovery patterns like "list all tiddlers tagged X" where X is a custom tag - Looking up layout templates, app definitions, tool registrations, or config tiddlers shipped by plugins When plain `[tag[]]` is fine: - Searching only user-created content (e.g., diary entries, notes) - Contexts where you know all matching tiddlers are in the main store Symptoms of this bug: - "No items found" even though tagged tiddlers exist inside a plugin - Dynamic lists that work after editing (overriding) a shadow tiddler but not before - Features that work in dev (where .tid files are real tiddlers) but break in single-file wiki (where they're shadows) "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-slots":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-slots","created":"20260329120000000","modified":"20260329120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/slots","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/slots","llm-help-description":"$slot/$fill scope limitations — no colons in names, procedure scope, @scope CSS","llm-help-sort":"05","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"$slot / $fill Gotchas 1. No colons in slot/fill names `$name="view:sco"` does NOT match between `$slot` and `$fill`. TW silently fails — no error, just falls back to default content. ``` <!-- BROKEN — colon breaks matching --> <$slot $name="view:sco"/> + <$fill $name="view:sco"> <!-- CORRECT — use hyphens --> <$slot $name="view-sco"/> + <$fill $name="view-sco"> ``` --- 2. $slot inside \procedure does NOT find $fill from outer $transclude $tiddler= This is the biggest gotcha. When a consumer transcludes a component tiddler with `$fill` content, `$slot` calls INSIDE a `\procedure` defined in that tiddler cannot find the fills. ``` <!-- Component tiddler: my-component.tid --> \procedure my-view(element) <$slot $name="my-slot" element=<<element>>>fallback</$slot> \end <!-- Body level: --> BODY: <$slot $name="my-slot">fallback</$slot> <!-- WORKS --> PROC: <$transclude $variable="my-view" element="x"/> <!-- DOES NOT WORK --> ``` Workarounds: - Put `$slot` at body level with `<%if%>` type branching (vtabs pattern): ``` <%if [<type>match[sec]]%> <$slot $name="view-sec" element=<<element>>><<element>></$slot> <%elseif [<type>match[act]]%> <$slot $name="view-act" element=<<element>>><<element>></$slot> <%endif%> ``` - Transclude an intermediate tiddler (pills pattern) — `$slot` works across `$transclude $tiddler=` boundaries but not through bare `$transclude $variable=` from body level. --- 3. @scope CSS is Chrome-only `@scope (.my-class) { ... }` does NOT work in Firefox. Use class-prefixed CSS instead: ```css /* BROKEN in Firefox */ @scope (.my-component) { span { color: red; } } /* CORRECT — works everywhere */ .my-component span { color: red; } ``` "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-titles":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-titles","created":"20260329120000000","modified":"20260329120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/titles","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/titles","llm-help-description":"Tiddler titles with spaces need [[double brackets]] — in filters, lists, tags, and tool calls","llm-help-sort":"01","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Titles with Spaces — [[Double Bracket]] Rules This is the single most common LLM mistake. Tiddler titles containing spaces MUST be wrapped in `[[double brackets]]` in most contexts. Without them, each word is treated as a separate title. When brackets are REQUIRED: - **Filter expressions**: `[title[My Tiddler]]` or `[[My Tiddler]]` — without brackets, `My` and `Tiddler` are separate filter runs - **List fields** (tags, list): `[[Tag One]] [[Tag Two]]` — without brackets, this is 4 separate tags - **`$list` filter parameter**: `filter="[[My Tiddler]]"` — bare `My Tiddler` is two separate titles - **Tool calls** (`create_tiddler`, `set_field`, etc.): title parameter must be the exact title string, but any filter-based lookup needs brackets - **`enlist` / `enlist-input`**: These split on spaces — `enlist[[My Tiddler]]` returns two items `My` and `Tiddler` - **`tag[]` operator**: `[tag[My Tag]]` works (operator parameter is a single string), but listing results in a `tags` field needs brackets: `[[My Tag]] [[Other Tag]]` When brackets are NOT needed: - **Widget attributes**: `$tiddler="My Tiddler"` — quoted attributes are single strings - **`title` field value**: `title: My Tiddler` in a `.tid` file — the field value is the entire line - **`$action-navigate $to="My Tiddler"`** — widget attribute, not a filter - **`{{My Tiddler!!field}}`** — transclusion syntax handles spaces (but `{{My Tiddler}}` without `!!field` is fine too) Common mistakes: ``` <!-- BROKEN — opens "Project" and "Overview" as two tiddlers --> <$action-navigate $to=Project Overview/> <!-- CORRECT --> <$action-navigate $to="Project Overview"/> <!-- BROKEN — filter returns two items: "My" and "Tiddler" --> [My Tiddler] <!-- CORRECT --> [[My Tiddler]] <!-- BROKEN — tags field has 4 tags instead of 2 --> tags: Tag One Tag Two <!-- CORRECT --> tags: [[Tag One]] [[Tag Two]] <!-- BROKEN — enlist splits on spaces --> <$list filter="[enlist[alpha beta My Tiddler]]"> <!-- produces: alpha, beta, My, Tiddler (4 items) --> <!-- CORRECT — use enlist with proper bracketing --> <$list filter="[enlist[alpha beta [[My Tiddler]]]]"> <!-- produces: alpha, beta, My Tiddler (3 items) --> ``` Best practice: When in doubt, ALWAYS use brackets around titles. `[[simple-title]]` works fine even when brackets aren't strictly needed. Title naming conventions to avoid the problem: - Use hyphens or slashes: `my-project-overview`, `projects/overview` - Use CamelCase: `ProjectOverview` - System tiddlers: `$:/config/my/setting` (no spaces) --- Titles with `/` after a file extension break filesystem sync: When a tiddler title appends `/child` to a title that ends with a file extension (e.g., `.pdf`), the filesystem adapter tries to create a directory where the file already exists. This causes `ENOTDIR` errors in a retry loop. ``` <!-- BROKEN — filesystem can't create report.pdf/extracted.tid because report.pdf is a file --> My Report.pdf/extracted My Report.pdf/summaries/model-timestamp <!-- CORRECT — use dot instead of slash for child tiddlers of file-backed tiddlers --> My Report.pdf.extracted My Report.pdf.summaries/model-timestamp ``` This applies to any tiddler whose title matches a real file on disk (common with `_canonical_uri` tiddlers for uploaded files, PDFs, etc.). "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-wikitext":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas-wikitext","created":"20260329120000000","modified":"20260329120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas/wikitext","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas/wikitext","llm-help-description":"Wikitext markup pitfalls — $reveal fragmentation, code-body, comments, $let vs $set, p-wrapping, \define substitution migration","llm-help-sort":"03","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"Wikitext Gotchas 1. `$reveal` cannot fragment HTML structure `$reveal` renders as a widget node. You CANNOT use it to conditionally open/close HTML tags across boundaries — TW's widget tree doesn't support loose closing/opening tags inside widget bodies. ``` <!-- BROKEN — renders literal </div><div> text --> <$reveal type="match" state="..." text="split"> <div class="pane"> </$reveal> <!-- CORRECT — use CSS class toggle --> <$let layoutClass={{{[[$:/state/layout]get[text]match[split]then[split-class]]}}}> <div class=<<layoutClass>>> <div class="pane-a">...content...</div> <div class="pane-b">...content...</div> </div> </$let> ``` Key principle: Always keep HTML structure intact within a single widget scope. Use CSS classes to change layout modes. --- 2. `code-body: yes` disables pragma parsing With `code-body: yes`, TW treats the entire body as literal content — `\import`, `\procedure`, `\function` at the top are NOT parsed as pragmas. They become visible text. - **Use `code-body: yes`** for tiddlers with NO pragmas (simple templates, pure HTML/wikitext) - **Omit `code-body: yes`** if the tiddler uses `\import`, `\procedure`, `\function`, or any pragma Symptom: headings render as literal `!! Title` because a preceding `\import` wasn't consumed as a pragma. --- 3. `<%-- --%>` is NOT a valid comment TW has NO wikitext comment syntax `<%-- ... --%>`. This renders as literal text. The `<%if%>` / `<%else%>` / `<%endif%>` conditional syntax exists, but there is no comment variant. ``` <!-- BROKEN — renders as visible text --> <%-- this is not a comment --%> <!-- CORRECT — use HTML comments --> <!-- this is a comment --> ``` --- 4. Use `$let` instead of nested `$set` `$let` is more efficient (no tiddler context creation) and supports multiple variables: ``` <!-- Anti-pattern --> <$set name="a" value="1"><$set name="b" value="2">...</$set></$set> <!-- Better --> <$let a="1" b="2">...</$let> ``` `$set` is still needed for: `filter` attribute (computed value), `select` attribute, `tiddler`/`subtiddler` context. --- 5. TW wraps inline wikitext in `<p>` tags When wikitext content isn't in block mode, TW wraps it in `<p>`. This matters for test comparisons and layout. Use `$mode="block"` on `$transclude` when you need block-level rendering without `<p>` wrappers. --- 6. `<%if%>`/`<%else%>` + `invokeActions` bug Action widgets inside `<%else%>` branches are NOT reachable by `Widget.invokeActions()`. The else branch is stored in `elseChildren` which `invokeActions` does not traverse. ``` <!-- BROKEN — action in else branch never fires --> <%if [<condition>]%> <$action-log message="true"/> <%else%> <$action-log message="false"/> <%endif%> <!-- CORRECT — use $list with emptyMessage --> <$list filter="[<condition>]" variable="ignore"> <$action-log message="true"/> </$list> <$list filter="[<condition>]" emptyMessage="""<$action-log message="false"/>"""/> ``` Read-only rendering (not actions) works fine with `<%if%>`. --- 7. Nested `{{{ }}}` is invalid — filter expressions cannot nest `{{{...}}}` (filtered transclusion) cannot contain another `{{{...}}}`. The inner triple braces are not evaluated — they become literal text, producing broken output. ``` <!-- BROKEN — inner {{{...}}} is literal text --> <$button class={{{ [[prefix-]addsuffix{{{ [<x>match[y]then[active]] }}}] }}}> <!-- CORRECT — use $let to pre-compute the inner value --> <$let suffix={{{[<x>match[y]then[active]]}}} cls={{{[[prefix-]addsuffix<suffix>]}}}> <$button class=<<cls>>> </$let> <!-- CORRECT — combine into one filter expression --> <$button class={{{ [<x>match[y]then[prefix-active]else[prefix-]] }}}> ``` Rule: If you need a computed value inside a filter, break it into a `$let` chain. Each `{{{...}}}` must be a standalone, non-nested expression. --- 8. `{{{ }}}` only works in widget attributes — not in `<<procedure>>` calls Filtered transclusion `{{{...}}}` is valid inside widget attributes like `<$widget attr={{{ filter }}}/>`. It does NOT work as an argument to a `<<procedure>>` macro-style call. ``` <!-- BROKEN — {{{ }}} is not evaluated in procedure calls --> <<my-procedure arg:{{{ [<x>get[field]] }}}>> <!-- CORRECT — use $transclude to call the procedure with computed args --> <$transclude $variable="my-procedure" arg={{{ [<x>get[field]] }}}/> <!-- CORRECT — or pre-compute with $let --> <$let val={{{ [<x>get[field]] }}}> <<my-procedure arg:<<val>>>> </$let> ``` Rule: `<<procedure>>` calls only accept literal values and `<<variable>>` references. For computed arguments, always use `<$transclude $variable="name" arg={{{ filter }}}/>` instead. --- 9. `\define` substitution syntax does NOT work in `\procedure` or `\function` `\define` performs textual substitution BEFORE parsing: `$param$` inserts a parameter value, `$(varname)$` inserts an outer variable. `\procedure` and `\function` do NOT support either form — they become literal text. Never use `\define`. Always use `\procedure`, `\function`, or `\widget`. When migrating: ``` <!-- OLD — \define with $param$ and $(var)$ substitution --> \define my-state() $:/state/view/$(currentTiddler)$/mode \define my-filter(type) [rrt.type[$type$]sort[title]] <!-- NEW — \function with filter operators --> \function my-state() [<currentTiddler>addprefix[$:/state/view/]addsuffix[/mode]] \function my-filter(type) [rrt.type<__type__>sort[title]] ``` Migration rules by context: - **Computed paths** (`$:/state/foo/$(var)$/bar`): use `\function` with `addprefix`/`addsuffix` - **Parameter access** (`$param$`): use `<<param>>` in procedure bodies, `<__param__>` in filter expressions - **Nested macro refs** (`$(otherMacro)$`): use `\function` with `[function[otherMacro]]` chaining - **Filter string construction** (building `[title[$(var)$]]` for subfilter): inline backtick template at the call site — backtick templates (`` `...$(var)$...` ``) support `$(var)$` regardless of pragma type Exception: Inside backtick template strings (`` `...` ``), `$(var)$` substitution works with any pragma type. This is a separate mechanism from `\define`. "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/pages/tw-gotchas","created":"20260329120000000","modified":"20260329120000000","tags":"$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page","llm-help-path":"tw-gotchas","llm-help-context":"tw-gotchas","llm-help-description":"Common TiddlyWiki pitfalls for LLMs — titles, filters, wikitext, shadows, slots, kin filter","llm-help-sort":"02","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"TiddlyWiki Gotchas Hard-won lessons about TiddlyWiki syntax and behavior. Read these BEFORE writing wikitext, filters, or creating tiddlers — they will save you from the most common mistakes. Categories: - **titles** — Tiddler titles with spaces, double-bracket syntax, list fields - **filters** — Filter expression pitfalls (short-circuit, `:filter`, chaining, variables) - **filter-runs** — How `~`, `+`, `:filter`, `:map` interact across runs (else, pipe, scope, `:map` single-result, interleaving) - **wikitext** — Markup gotchas (`$reveal`, `code-body`, comments, `$let` vs `$set`) - **shadows** — Shadow tiddler discovery (`[all[tiddlers+shadows]...]`) - **slots** — `$slot`/`$fill` scope limitations, naming restrictions - **cascades** — ViewTemplateBodyFilter `:cascade` ordering, third-party plugins breaking the chain - **hooks** — Hook chain fragility (`return tiddler`), change event fallback, draft safety - **kin-filter** — `[kin[]]` tree traversal: direction semantics, cache staleness, depth off-by-one Navigate deeper: llm_help("tw-gotchas/titles"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/filters"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/filter-runs"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/wikitext"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/shadows"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/slots"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/cascades"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/hooks"), llm_help("tw-gotchas/kin-filter") "},"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/readme":{"title":"$:/plugins/rimir/llm-help/readme","created":"20260327120000000","modified":"20260327120000000","type":"text/vnd.tiddlywiki","text":"! LLM Help A self-discoverable, hierarchical help system that lets LLMs browse context-aware documentation to solve wiki-specific tasks autonomously. !! How it works The plugin provides a single MCP tool `llm_help` that LLMs call to navigate a tree of help pages: * `llm_help()` -- returns top-level help categories * `llm_help("data-model")` -- navigates deeper into a category * `llm_help("data-model/person/create")` -- reaches a specific help page Each level returns child topics with descriptions, so the LLM decides where to go based on its current task. Help pages can contain dynamic content -- rendered at call time from the wiki's live state (e.g., typed data schemas, existing instances). !! Key features * ''Hierarchical navigation'' -- help pages organized in a tree of any depth * ''Dynamic content'' -- pages rendered as wikitext at call time, pulling live data from the wiki * ''Context-aware'' -- pages can be scoped to specific contexts (e.g., an appify app) * ''Multi-path mounting'' -- same page reachable from multiple paths in the tree * ''Plugin-extensible'' -- any plugin can contribute help pages via tagged tiddlers * ''User shortcuts'' -- users can add quick-reference help pages via the settings UI * ''Context selector'' -- settings popup to toggle active context keys with page counts * ''Help browser'' -- settings tab lets users see and navigate what the LLM sees !! For plugin authors To add help pages from your plugin, create shadow tiddlers with: * Tag: `$:/tags/rimir/llm-help/page` * Field `llm-help-path`: navigation path (space-separated for multiple paths) * Field `llm-help-description`: one-line description shown in parent listings * Optional `llm-help-sort`: numeric sort order within siblings * Optional `llm-help-context`: space-separated context keys (page hidden when context doesn't match) Pages with no `llm-help-context` are always visible. Pages with a context are only visible when the active context matches. !! Requirements * [[$:/plugins/rimir/llm-connect]] -- provides the MCP tool infrastructure * [[$:/plugins/rimir/theme]] -- provides UI styling classes 12px 6px; font-weight: 600; } .llmh-ctx-option { padding: 3px 12px; } .llmh-ctx-option label { cursor: pointer; } .llmh-ctx-clear { border-top: 1px solid <<colour tiddler-border>>; margin-top: 4px; { margin-top: 0; } .llmh-shortcut-form input, .llmh-shortcut-form textarea { width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; } .llmh-shortcut-form textarea { min-height: 80px; font-family: monospace; font-size:
